title: Flowing zinc slurry for long-duration energy storage
authors: Wenyong Chen
Yanyan Wang
Zhiqi Liu
Fengmei Wang
Xinjie Li
Zihao Zhang
Wei Li
Fang Fang
Changkun Zhang
Renchao Che
Dalin Sun
Fei Wang
abstract: Published online: 24 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41560-026-02091-w Aqueous Zn batteries are attractive for low-cost and safe energy storage, yet their practical use is hindered by limited reversibility. Here the authors report a ligand-confined, flowable Zn slurry that enables durable and efficient operation.

title: A Hybrid Electrolyte-Based Reversible Direct Carbon Fuel Cell for In-Situ CO2 Utilization and Energy Conversion
authors: Jiale Kong, Yidong Jiang and Yixiang Shi
abstract: Harnessing Martian atmospheric CO2 for in situ energy conversion and storage is crucial for sustainable extraterrestrial exploration. Solid carbon outperforms gaseous media as an energy carrier, offering superior <mark>energy density</mark> and storage stability. Herein, we demonstrate a novel hybrid electrolyte-based reversible direct carbon fuel cell (R-DCFC) capable of cyclic CO2-to-carbon for energy storage and in situ power generation. This cycle is realized in a blind tubular cell configuration with the YSZ/molten carbonate hybrid electrolyte. At 800 °C, the cell achieved stable CO2 electrolysis (2.5…

title: The ZIF-67-Derived CoS2/WS2 Mott-Schottky Heterojunction Facilitates Electrocatalytic Oxygen Evolution
authors: Yumiao Zhang, Tengrui Feng, Manhua Liao and Qi Wu
abstract: Developing non-precious metal electrocatalysts with high activity and low <mark>overpotential</mark> for the oxygen evolution reaction (OER) is essential for minimizing energy consumption in electrochemical water splitting toward hydrogen production. In this study, a bimetallic sulfide Mott-Schottky heterojunction (CoS2/WS2) was synthesized via a solvothermal method using a metal-organic framework (MOF) as the precursor. CoS2/WS2 delivers the overpotentials of 326 mV for OER at 10 mA cm−2 in 1.0 M KOH electrolyte and high stability, retaining their catalytic properties even after 140 h of continuous use. D…

title: Alumina Nanoparticles-Driven Effect of Optical and Ionic Properties in PVA-MgCl2 Polymer Electrolytes
authors: Ashwini A. B., R. F. Bhajantri, Vijaykumar C. J., Soumya S. Bulla, Chetan Chavan and Vipin Cyriac
abstract: Advanced solid-state polymer electrolytes are essential for next-generation batteries and optical systems. Using the solution casting technique (SCT), this study effectively produced polyvinyl alcohol (PVA)-based polymer nanocomposite electrolytes to study how integrating salt and nanoparticles changed their structure, optical, dielectric, and ionic properties. Five samples were made: Pure PVA (P1), , and that had 1, 5, and 10 nanoparticles added to it (P3-P5). X-ray diffraction (XRD) showed that the addition of fillers caused a controlled change between crystalline and amorphous phases. Addin…
